channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Last-minute dispute in ceasefire talks between Israel and Hamas has been resolved, officials say
item.description: DOHA, Qatar (AP) — Qatari and Hamas officials say a last-minute dispute in ceasefire talks between Israel and Hamas has been resolved. Israel announced late Wednesday that Hamas had tried to change agreed-upon understandings for security arrangements along Gaza’s border with Egypt.
item.dc:creator: NAJIB JOBAIN, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden to address the nation days before Trump's inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday, days before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires burning homes and businesses in Los Angeles for a week have killed at least 25 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 buildings in what might be the most expensive set of conflagrations in the nation's history. The blazes started Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US inflation picked up in December but underlying price pressures eased
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. inflation accelerated last month as prices for gas, eggs, and used cars rose, yet underlying price pressures also showed signs of easing, bolstering hopes that the Federal Reserve could still cut its key interest rate this year.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass posed for photos at a cocktail party as Palisades Fire exploded
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass is at the center of controversy after social media photos showed her posing at a cocktail party in Ghana as the Palisades Fire exploded.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nature photographer charged with migrant smuggling
item.description: A woman who says she drove her SUV off the beaten path to take photographs of the stunning southern New Mexico landscape is facing smuggling charges.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: FDA proposes sweeping limits on nicotine in cigarettes
item.description: It's an 11th-hour plan from the Biden administration that will take years to go into effect, if it happens at all.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Fire tornadoes a risk under California's extreme wildfire conditions
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How wireless companies keep signals alive in wildfires
item.description: Communications companies are stepping up to support communities impacted by the recent LA area wildfires with network reinforcements and it’s making a difference.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: New documentary reveals antisemitic hostage siege captured on camera at Texas synagogue
item.description: Unseen security footage from the 11-hour Colleyville synagogue hostage crisis, capturing terror and resilience, is shown for the first time in a new documentary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: Wildfires latest: A final round of dangerous fire weather and dry conditions is in the forecast
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians were on edge as winds began picking up during a final round of dangerous fire weather forecast for the region where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FDA floats plan to make cigarettes nonaddictive, but its fate rests with Trump
item.description: The Biden administration is floating a long-awaited proposal to make cigarettes less addictive by capping their nicotine levels.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FAFSA forms to make October release after two-year struggle
item.description: The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) forms will be released on Oct. 1 this year, the Department of Education announced on Wednesday, hitting the normal deadline after two chaotic years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, a potential 2028 candidate, wants to find common ground with Trump
item.description: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer says she does not want to pick a fight with President-elect Donald Trump but won't back down from one.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti And Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: Preston Martin figured the retro blue Volkswagen van he slept in for a year during college was a goner, given that he parked it in a Malibu neighborhood just before the Palisades fire ripped through, reducing homes and cars to rubble and charred metal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SEC sues Elon Musk, saying he didn't disclose Twitter ownership on time before buying it
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has sued billionaire Elon Musk, saying he failed to disclose his ownership of Twitter stock in a timely manner in early 2022, before buying the social media site.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cardi B calls for investigation at federal prison in West Virginia
item.description: "THEY ALL KNOW SHES CONNECTED TO ME AND I FEEL THEY DOING THAT TO BE SPITEFUL," Cardi B 's post said.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, NewsNation

item.title: Octavia Butler imagined LA ravaged by fires. Her Altadena cemetery survived
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — Decades ago, the writer Octavia Butler had imagined a Los Angeles ravaged by fires. The Altadena cemetery where the science fiction and Afrofuturism author is buried did catch fire last week but suffered “minimal damage,” according to a statement on the cemetery's web site.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE and CHRIS PIZZELLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC looks to cut emissions from charboilers at restaurants
item.description: Under the proposed rule, restaurants with charbroil grills would need to install an emissions control device.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 100-year-old pipeline deal disrupts Round Rock neighborhood
item.description: "I've been living here almost 30 years, and that's when they came out here. And they just showed up one day out of the blue," Mehrabian said.
item.dc:creator: Mercedez Hernandez, NewsNation

item.title: Flu hospitalizations have doubled in Colorado since late December
item.description: "We took a big jump after Dec. 21, and it's still going up," said Dr. Ned Calonge, chief medical officer at the Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment.
item.dc:creator: Kim Posey, NewsNation

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This popular hiking trail may hold clues to how Palisades Fire ignited
item.description: A beloved hiking spot north of Sunset Boulevard in Pacific Palisades is reportedly a point of interest for investigators looking into the origins of the costliest wildfire in the history of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Girls basketball coach accused of hitting person with car after argument
item.description: A girls' youth basketball coach is accused of hitting a person with his vehicle after an argument following a game Saturday in Columbia, according to state police, and it was caught on surveillance video.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KRON4

item.title: Vulnerable Americans live in the shadow of COVID-19 as most move on
item.description: People with immune system health problems continue to take precautions against COVID-19 five years into the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Devna Bose And Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Patrick and Brittany Mahomes welcome third child
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes and his wife Brittany Mahomes have had a busy bye week
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Anita Bryant, singer known for opposing gay rights, dies at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant, a former Miss Oklahoma, Grammy-nominated singer and prominent booster of orange juice and other products who became known over the second half of her life for her outspoken opposition to gay rights, has died. She was 84.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: More firefighters rush to Los Angeles ahead of stronger winds
item.description: A growing force of firefighters and equipment moved into the Los Angeles area as another round of powerful winds threatened to trigger new wildfires
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5
